36052.name: In the Tide-pools
36052.description: The salt water stings your eyes as you scan the pool, blinding you for a moment before the bright gaudy colors refresh your vision. Luminescent slime on the rocks lights this chamber, casting a dim greenish glow over everything. You flinch away from several long strands of seaweed as they brush your arm, startling the pink sea anemone into hiding. Blushing at your callous reactions, you swim with more caution and respect to the inhabitants. A mold- green sea cucumber bumps into your arm as it slowly trudges down the rock you've been leaning on.

36053.name: In the Tide-pools
36053.description: Electric blue and yellow fish whiz by your face in schools, staying at a safe distance from your alien presence. Dark brown eels hang long, sinuous bodies out of holes in the rock, twisting around, their mouths wrinkled up like an old woman's. The green and pink sea anemones unfurl their soft looking but dangerous tentacles allowing tiny green cleaning fish to dart among the poisonous fronds. Entire walls of the cavernous tide-pool are covered with mussels, abalone, and hungry starfish.

36096.name: Royal Courtyard
36096.description: Torches line the walk, keeping the enclosure warm and well lit. The people are easy-going and happy, but there seems to be an unusually large number of soldiers patrolling the area and surrounding forests. Several soldiers walk by carrying armfuls of wooden poles toward the weapon maker.

36097.name: Ke'ali's Trinkets
36097.description: Golden dried grass lies scattered across the floor, giving off a fresh clean scent. Ke'ali sits behind a counter laden with flowers, wax, and strands of fiber. The walls are covered with pegs, some empty, others holding leis, bracelets, and other such bits of jewelry. The shop is a little warm for your comfort, but buckets of cold water stand in each corner to keep the vegetation fresh.

36098.name: Royal Courtyard
36098.description: The courtyard expands over a fair amount of land, with a long woven mat leading up to the enclosure. Well-groomed women chatter among themselves, keeping an eye on the rambunctious children running around in senseless games. A fat, smiling woman keeps shop to the south.

36099.name: Royal Gateway
36099.description: Tall sharpened posts fence in the royal inhabitants of the island. A constant breeze wafts over the gates and cools the yard. Decorative feathers and shells hang in brilliant profusion from oil torches set in the ground. The sounds of music and laughter from farther back in the compound reach your ears, making your heart beat faster with the hypnotic pulse of drums. The gateway opens up further to the east, with small groups of jostling, gossiping people.

36004.name: In the Moist Undergrowth
36004.description: Bright orange and blue bird of paradise grow in huge clumps, housing beautifully intricate spiderwebs. Your movement is hindered as you rustle chest deep in ferns and wild grass. A light mist floats around, muffling the calls of birds in the moss covered trees. The stench of an unwashed body drifts to you from somewhere close by.

36005.name: In the Moist Undergrowth
36005.description: Tender green ferns caress your arms as you push your way through thick grass and brush. A thin mist brushes in from the east, leaving droplets of condensation on your eyelashes. The world around you is alive with the sounds of birds singing and wild pigs rooting in the brush.

36006.name: In the Tropical Forest
36006.description: The air smells fresh and clean, purified by the frequent showers of the island. You crush your way through vegetation, disturbing insects and animals alike. Your way is free in all directions.

36007.name: In the Tropical Forest
36007.description: A small swarm of gnats hover around your face in a thick cloud. The humidity of the forest is still bearable, but flowers and trees offer no comfort for the sweat running down your neck and back.

36008.name: In the Tropical Forest
36008.description: The sound of the ocean is loud here, mixed with the cries of restless sea birds. A few of the larger trees have trunks edged with a thin white layer of salt. The grass here is not the soft breed of the inner island, but the coarse, tough survivor of the salty air.

36009.name: In the Tropical Forest
36009.description: Small fruit trees grow in random places with evidence of rooting pigs among the roots. The path to the north seems to spread out into less crowded land, as opposed to the dense forest around you.

36016.name: Open Clearing
36016.description: Hard packed earth is broken only by small weeds and clumps of grass. Tiny hoof prints etch the surface in a confused mass. A large granite rock stands to the east of you, but the clearing is open to the north and west of you. Moist air emanates from the forest to the south.

36017.name: Open Clearing
36017.description: A herd of slender deer graze peacefully on tough, salty grass around you. The few that notice you keep a watchful eye on your movements. To the north is a small farm with a stream running through it, the signs of a taro farm.

36018.name: Open Clearing
36018.description: Except for the herd of deer wandering around, the clearing is fairly empty. A dusty little strawberry bush seems to have been overlooked by the foraging herds. The only break in the peaceful quiet is the sound of dozens of little mouths munching.

36019.name: Open Clearing
36019.description: A large orchid blooms in a violent pink from the cropped grass. Upon inspecting the grass a little more, you discover a small deer hollow that's still warm to the touch. To the west lies the ocean. To the north is a small farm with a stream running through it, the signs of a taro farm.

36020.name: Taro Paddy
36020.description: A clear stream runs through several of the paddies, keeping the water level over the fat greyish purple tubers. Swarms of gnats drift around your unwashed body and attempt to land in your ears and eyes. Silver winged dragonflies zip past in pursuit of the bothersome insects. The mud of the taro farm is too thick to walk directly through, but the residents of the farmhouse have a well-used dirt road heading north toward the central area of the island and south to the main village.

36021.name: Women's Dining Room
36021.description: A long, low table has been set in the center of the room. Several bowls and platters of fine refreshments, decorated with fresh flowers, are filled with fruits and meats. The only thing more welcome than food to your hungry eyes is the presence of the several lovely ladies watching you with smiling faces.

36022.name: A Bamboo Thatched Hut
36022.description: Mats woven out of river rushes line the floor, keeping dust down to a minimum. Rolls of bedding stand in neat stacks in a corner of the room wrapped in blankets of beaten kapa. Smoke from the cooking fire drifts in from outside the door, bringing in the delicious smell of baking pig. The gutted bodies of fish await slicing on a wooden board next to several tomatoes. Golden sunlight slants through gaps in the palm leaf walls, giving the room a cheerful air. Toward the back of the hut is a pile of bone needles and thread. Fragrant flowers have been separated into different bowls in accordance to the color.

36023.name: Open Clearing
36023.description: Lush grass has slowly begun to take over this edge of the field. Exotic flora sprout from wet spots in the ground. The forest is still to the south, but now the clearing opens in all other directions.

36024.name: Clearings Edge
36024.description: Sand from the westward side of the island mixes with the fine dust of the clearing. Wrinkled husks of uncultivated yams sift up from the ground, covered with a fine layer of white salt. A herd of deer snuff the earth for edibles.

36025.name: Tool Hut
36025.description: On entering and looking around, maybe this hut is closer to a general work hut. Yes, tools do line the walls, but strings of drying flowers hang from the rafters and chickens scatter away from your stomping feet.

36026.name: Dirt Path
36026.description: The path becomes increasingly overgrown as you look further south. Scattered sprouts of dandelions break up the neatly beaten path. A mango tree drops its soft sweet fruit in sticky messes all over the side of the path, attracting field mice and insects aplenty. A tall pole with a ball of pure white kapa marks the road as property of the sovereign moi of the island.

36027.name: The Common Road
36027.description: Small packs of dogs trot by with wagging tails and lolling tongues. A group of boys run past, one pushing a thin wooden wheel with a stick while the others throw dull spears through the spinning center. Young women carry large baskets of shellfish and seaweed in from the beach and head toward the main village.

36028.name: Kapa Beating
36028.description: As they bend over their work, each one croons softly to herself and the end result is a full, warm song of content. The woven huts are closer now, each section of land marked by low bamboo fences.

36029.name: Main Road
36029.description: The road has been beaten down to a shiny surface from all the traffic running through the houses. The clean air gives credit to the native habits of relieving themselves far from any public areas. Many huts have collected in this general area, but the main palace seems to be farther ahead.

36030.name: Main Road
36030.description: Brightly clad men and women walk leisurely along the road, browsing among the small tables children have set up to sell shellfish and various kinds of fruit. The children holler songs proclaiming the freshness and superiority of their goods over anyone else's. Occasionally, a parent stops by to replenish a child's wares. The main road continues north and south.

36031.name: Main Road
36031.description: Fat banana trees shade the road and sweeten the air with the warm scent of their fruit. Piglets root in the soft mud in the gutter, splattering it into the air. A ruffled chicken squawks angrily as she runs from a knife-wielding chef.

36032.name: Main Road
36032.description: Bundles of coarse twine have been stacked neatly on a short wooden bench to be close at hand for a quick mend. Bits of palm fibers fallen from the overhanging trees bite into your feet from beneath the soft sand. The sharp sweet smell of an old pipe cuts through the air. Up ahead to the north is the palace. The main road continues south.

36033.name: End of the Main Road
36033.description: The outside of the royal enclosure could be well compared to that of a fortress. Tall poles, each as thick around as a man's waist, make up the fence. Sentries keep vigil around the walls, joking with neighbors, but always keeping a sharp eye out for any trouble. The main road ends here, but a small path for guards and soldiers leads around the enclosure.

36034.name: Fisherman's Hut
36034.description: A strong fishy odor pushes its way down your nose and into your clothes. Embarrassed by your weak stomach, you search around for a handkerchief or something to filter the smell. Bales of twine lie around the young woman's feet, slowly unraveling as she works.

36035.name: The Yellow Hut
36035.description: This hut is obviously new. Light shines off the fresh reeds covering the floor and the walls are tightly woven with no patches in them. Even the dust motes in the air sparkle. A crudely carved calabash stands in the corner next to the neatly rolled sleeping mats.

36036.name: Birthing Hut
36036.description: Lucky for you, it's empty right now. Like the other huts, there is very little ornamentation, only a few sleeping mats rolled up against the wall. An oil lamp tilts precariously on the window shelf. Rumors have reached the mainland that some women are forced to go into the mountains to give birth in the wild. Apparently, those stories are false.

36037.name: Stone Breakers
36037.description: A long man-made wall of stone extends far out into the ocean, blocking off further access to the northern part of the island by water. Tall waves crash into the breakers with surprising force; the tide-pools and reefs to the south probably wouldn't have much of a chance for survival if this weren't in place.

36038.name: Beach Edge
36038.description: A brisk, chill air ruffles your clothing and hair as you stroll along the beach. The cloudless skies are filled with life, as terns and gulls squawk and fight over tidbits. Tough yellow-green sea grass grows down to the high tide line, where piles of seaweed provide shelter to millions of tiny flies. The vegetation grows increasingly thick as you move north, eventually ending in a thick forest. Near the shore, a large tent can be seen with tons of foot traffic having disturbed the sands recently.

36039.name: Tide-pools
36039.description: Everything is covered with a thin green film of slime. Tiny reddish crabs click their claws together and bubble eagerly. Rippling yellow spotted eels poke their heads out of holes in the walls. Looking into the pool is hypnotic; everything is moving.

36040.name: Tide-pools
36040.description: Guano stained rocks and lava formations cradle pools which literally swim with life. From the surface, everything is brown and pebbly, but upon closer inspection everything is alive. Some of the rocks are actually sea anemones folded over themselves. Other rocks are covered with colonies of limpets and mussels.

36041.name: Shallow Waters
36041.description: A firm platform of bamboo shafts has been built at the shoreline for incoming boats. A few casual swimmers glide through the water with easy strokes. Down the coast a bit is a group of surf riders and canoe racers. Smooth grey rocks dot the sand, providing cover for the scuttling red crabs. The island expands northward from here, but there looks to be several tide- pools toward the western edge of the island. Despite a smooth landing, your footing is a little shaky; too much time out to sea.

36042.name: Surging Waves
36042.description: A rolling wave pushes you toward the small shoreline of the island. Half buried in the sands of the shallow water are various shells and clams. The warm waters splash over the edges of your craft and wet your feet. A good landing platform is to the west.

36043.name: Calm Water
36043.description: A high sheer cliff stares out to the east, awaiting the rising sun each day. It leads around at least half the island, with waterfalls cascading down into the ocean. Sharp rocks jut out of the water, but they should be easy enough to avoid for a comfortable landing.

36044.name: Coral Reef
36044.description: Rough coral cuts out of the water, creating natural channels in the water for smooth passage. Watch your hands and don't touch it though. Small eels and prickly animals hide in the crevices. Since the mountains of the island hug the ocean so closely on this half of the island, there is little point in tracing further north.

36045.name: Coral Reef
36045.description: A school of silver needle-like fish dart beneath the surface of the water, sending flashes into your eyes. The grey cliffs of the island to the west are carpeted with an emerald green. Golden coral prevents passage east.

36047.name: Natural Spring
36047.description: Cool water bubbles up from a crevice in the rocks. It tinkles down the incline of the island to the south in a muddy little stream bed. A goose splashes around merrily.

36048.name: Sugar Fields
36048.description: It is fairly difficult to move around the field with all the dead vegetation pulling at your feet. The sugar cane towers several feet above your head, rendering it impossible to see anything but sky. The warm brown smell of manure tickles your nose and settles into your clothes.

36049.name: Landing Point
36049.description: Dry palm branches shuffle in the ocean breeze. The sweet smell of green plants cleanses your nostrils of salty crust. A few other boats of varying size and make are tied to posts buried deep in the sand. Friends and spectators lean back in the warm sands to watch and cheer as the surf riders and swimmers race each other back to shore.

36050.name: Overgrown Road
36050.description: Grass grows thickly out of puddles from potholes in the road. The only people who ever use this road anymore are the royal bird-catchers and patrolling warriors. Looking far to the west, you can spot a small patch of blue ocean peeping through clusters of trees.

36051.name: Tip of the Island
36051.description: The path becomes increasingly overgrown as you look further north. Scattered sprouts of dandelions break up the neatly beaten path. A mango tree drops its soft sweet fruit in sticky messes all over the side of the path, attracting field mice and insects aplenty. A tall pole with a ball of pure white kapa marks the road as property of the sovereign moi of the island.

36055.name: Narrow Path
36055.description: A low spiked fence keeps the path at about one and a half feet wide, barely enough for a fully armed warrior to pass through. Stubby ends of broken branches poke out into your arms and legs. The road is perfectly void of all rocks and pebbles, making movement much smoother and quieter.

36056.name: Narrow Path
36056.description: Thick strips of leather keep the fence in good repair, but there are small gaps in it to allow special messengers in and out. A stubby candle rests in a sconce on the palace wall. It is covered, but ready to light a nearby signal fire.

36057.name: Narrow Path
36057.description: A scantily furnished guardhouse stands directly west of you. An off-duty sentry leans back against the wall, dozing fitfully in the warm air. Swarms of tiny gnats cloud around his sweaty face and stick to the dirt in his greasy hair.

36058.name: Guardhouse
36058.description: Hard grey slabs of granite tile the floor of the guardhouse. Your only explanation for this would be that the guards might use it to sharpen their weapons very conveniently. An odd thought, but the long brown and silver streaks on parts of the stone seem to prove your point. A large pile of tinder is on the slate roof, ready to be lit for an alarm.

36450.name: Inside the Tent
36450.description: The tent is made up simply of tattered cloth draped over large wooden poles connected by overhead beams to block the wind. A couple tables divide the tent from north to south and is covered with various papers, scrolls, books and a large map of the archipelago. An assortment of equipment racks are scattered around the perimeter of the tent.

36077.name: Storage Chamber
36077.description: Cool air and the smell of fresh sawdust fills your nostrils. Barrels of salted meat stand in one corner, huge wheels of cheese are stacked in another. The wood shavings keep mice and insects away from the sugar- cured hams sitting on the high shelves. A jug of beer begs to be tasted with a good helping of meat and bread.

36078.name: Compost Heap
36078.description: Small bits of trash lay scattered around the edge of the tree-line. The surface of a large black pool ripples despite a lack of wind. There are no birds to be seen here and even the trees keep a fair distance away from the center of the copse.

36079.name: Kitchen
36079.description: The kitchen is a huge room with half of the roof taken out to allow smoke to escape. A smoking pit has been set up across the room from the large bread ovens. The faces of the cooks are slick with sweat as they move from one pot to another pan, in a constant rush for the perfect feast.

36080.name: Servant's Hall
36080.description: Women hunch over their various tasks: folding, ironing, mending, and flower arranging. As they work they sing chants written by the priests of the island. One woman glances up to smile at you as she dips her fingertips into a bowl of melted tallow to waterproof a cloak.

36081.name: Men's Quarters
36081.description: The interior is fairly spartan, the walls bare of all fripperies. Wooden racks line the back, filled with shark-toothed knives and short javelins. A bundle of poles lie in the corner, ready to be sharpened and hardened in a fire. Embers burn with small flickering flames in the center of the room, burning off of shavings, leather ties, stone chips and reed fragments.

36082.name: Hua's Council Room
36082.description: Before you is a magnificent scene of barbaric royalty. Lower-classed chiefs rub elbows with the elite priests who watch the auguries for the island's future. King Hua, Moi of the island himself, reclines on a raised dias of red sandstone. A low murmur fills

36083.name: Women's Quarters
36083.description: Large tinted feathers add a frilly air to the room, framing the windows and mounted mirrors. A large chest in the corner overflows with soft material and clothing. Unlike most other dwellings here, the beds are unrolled along the walls and on top of wooden planks.

36085.name: Palace Hallway
36085.description: The hall splits in three different directions heading east. To the northeast is the women's quarters and eating area. Directly east is the king's private chambers, and to the southeast is the men's quarters and eating area. The women's area seems to be much fancier than even the king's private chambers. Eunuchs stand guard before the women's door and the entrance to the audience hall.

36086.name: Palace Hallway
36086.description: A troupe of dancers stand off to the side, primping themselves and each other in preparation for a performance. Soft brown necks are draped with sweet smelling leis as dainty ankles are wrapped with strings of shell. A middle aged man keeps an eye on passers-by, making sure no performer gets harassed. From the south drifts the smell of roasted pig. The entire palace seems to be preparing for both a war and a festival.

36087.name: Palace Hall
36087.description: Parakeets squawk noisily from bamboo cages suspended from beams in the ceiling. The palace is kept scrupulously clean, and the floors completely covered by woven mats or slabs of a beige sandstone. Fresh air and light come in from openings set in the high ceiling. Servants move around you in a constant bustle.

36088.name: Servant's Entrance
36088.description: A thin stream of dirty water runs from under the doorway into the dirt of the entrance. Taro tops and pig entrails scattered in the bushes give evidence that the servants just throw the garbage out the back door to fertilize the thick grass growing against the bamboo fence.

36089.name: South of the Fountain
36089.description: The sounds of water splashing come from the north. Small children play tag with each other, dashing around you in dizzying circles. A pleasant warm aroma comes from the south, similar to baking bananas.

36090.name: Ke'ali's Workroom
36090.description: Smoothly polished boxes overflow with bright feathers and colored threads. A small cup on the table holds bone needles for heavy duty work and silver needles for fine embroidery. Wooden toys lie in various stages of construction on the floor, some even with knives still buried deep in the wood.

36091.name: Weapons Barter
36091.description: Neat little boxes of poison line the counter edge, right next to the small throwing knives. A bundle of javelins stands in the back corner, ready for use. As you carefully take stock of the craftsmanship of the various weapons, the slight change in the air causes you to turn and notice Kennak standing right beside you.

36092.name: Palace Entrance
36092.description: A thick mat woven of rushes covers the entrance to the palace. Tall wooden carvings stand next to the doorways, leering with huge open mouths. Small palm trees decorate the corners, framing the finely crafted wall hangings. Judging from the distinct patterns and colors, these hangings were not made anywhere on this island.

36093.name: Center of the Courtyard
36093.description: Four men sit in a small arc in the northern end of the courtyard, beating several percussion instruments for the troupe of dancers before them. A stout woman stands to the side, criticizing and teaching the dancers. A ring of torches circle the outer rim of the courtyard, opening out in rows to line the sidewalks.

36094.name: Communal Fountain
36094.description: A thick bamboo pipe runs water from a nearby stream into the huge stone basin before you. Naked children splash in the water and dance in the mist as their mothers bend to the laundry. You linger a little in hopes that one of the younger women might stop by to chat. The servants entrance is further south.

36095.name: The Jolly Pirate
36095.description: Most of these barrels contain water or dried food. A few of the barrels in the back give off a sweet alcoholic smell. Besides that, there's not that much of interest back here.
